metadata.description: CSA-in-a-Box business glossary. Defines key business concepts shared across domains.

terms.name: Customer
terms.short_description: Individual or organization that purchases products or services
terms.long_description: A Customer is identified by a unique customer_id that is consistent across all domains (sales, finance, shared). The Silver layer applies validation and standardization; the Gold layer provides dim_customers for analytics. PII fields (email, phone, address) are classified and monitored by Purview classification rules.

terms.name: Order
terms.short_description: Purchase transaction initiated by a customer
terms.long_description: An Order represents a commercial transaction. Orders flow through the medallion pipeline: raw ingestion (Bronze), validation and dedup (Silver), then dimensional modeling (Gold fact_orders). The is_valid flag in Silver marks quality issues without dropping rows.

terms.name: Product
terms.long_description: Products are categorized into Electronics, Clothing, Home, Books, and Sports. The Gold dim_products dimension adds a derived price_tier (economy, value, standard, premium) for analytics segmentation.

terms.name: Invoice
terms.short_description: Billing document issued to a customer
terms.long_description: Invoices link to sales Orders via order_id for revenue reconciliation. The finance domain owns invoice data from issuance through payment collection. The gld_aging_report classifies outstanding invoices into aging buckets for AR management.

terms.name: Payment
terms.short_description: Monetary transaction that settles an invoice
terms.long_description: Payments apply to invoices and can be partial. Multiple payments can settle a single invoice. The aging report computes outstanding_balance = invoice_total - sum(payments) to determine collection status.

terms.name: Revenue
terms.short_description: Income generated from sales of products and services
terms.long_description: Revenue is measured at two levels: (1) order revenue in the sales domain (total_amount in fact_orders) and (2) invoiced revenue in the finance domain (invoice_amount in slv_invoices). The gld_revenue_reconciliation model performs cross-domain reconciliation to identify discrepancies between the two measurements.

terms.name: Data Product
terms.short_description: Self-contained data asset with defined schema, SLA, and quality rules
terms.long_description: Data Products are the unit of data sharing in the Data Mesh architecture. Each product is defined by a contract.yaml file specifying schema, SLA (freshness, availability), quality rules, ownership, and access policies. Contracts live under domains/{domain}/data-products/{product}/contract.yaml.

terms.name: Medallion Architecture
terms.short_description: Three-layer data lakehouse pattern: Bronze, Silver, Gold
terms.long_description: Bronze: raw ingestion with minimal transformation (add metadata, no filtering). Silver: conformed data with type casting, dedup, and validation flags (is_valid + validation_errors). Key principle: flag-don't-drop, keeping all rows for audit. Gold: business-ready aggregates and dimensional models that filter to is_valid = true. Each layer is materialized as Delta Lake tables on ADLS Gen2.
